_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast commodity basket, an average index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices benchmarked to 2015 at 100, currently stands at 132, indicating a 32% increase since 2015. This rise has been uneven and influenced by recent volatility. The composite number encapsulates disparate movements across these key soft commodities. The index provides a broad measure but masks significant variation by individual commodity.
notes.movers: The largest contributors to the price increase are coffee, up 93% since 2015, and orange juice, up 22%. Wheat and sugar have seen relatively modest increases of 7% and 5%, respectively. These price changes reflect disruptions from weather events and crop diseases, such as frost and drought impacting arabica coffee regions and citrus greening affecting orange juice supply. Because soft commodity markets are thin, meaning they have less liquidity and smaller trading volumes, prices can swing sharply on these supply shocks rather than broad inflation trends.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is driven primarily by a few weather-sensitive and disease-affected crops rather than systemic inflation. Given the vulnerability of these specific commodities to climatic and biological factors, price levels can remain elevated for extended periods depending on recovery of crop yields. This underscores the importance of monitoring supply-side risks in soft commodity markets.
